title: Exposed container ports are not reachable from the same host when building the images and running the containers through GitHub Actions Self-hosted runners
body:
### Issue Description

```
$> podman-compose -v
podman-compose version: 1.0.6
['podman', '--version', '']
using podman version: 4.9.5
podman-compose version 1.0.6
podman --version 
podman version 4.9.5
exit code: 0
```

```
$> podman info

  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.33.12

  conmon:
    package: conmon-2.1.10-150500.9.9.1.x86_64

  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 30000

  networkBackend: netavark

    backend: netavark
    dns:
      package: aardvark-dns-1.12.2-150500.3.12.1.x86_64

      version: aardvark-dns 1.12.2

    version: netavark 1.12.2
  ociRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: runc-1.3.4-150000.88.1.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc

      runc version 1.3.4
      commit: v1.3.4-0-gd6d73eb8c602
      spec: 1.2.1
      go: go1.25.3

    apparmorEnabled: false
    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: true

    seccompProfilePath: /etc/containers/seccomp.json

  serviceIsRemote: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    package: slirp4netns-1.2.2-150600.1.5.x86_64
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.2.2

  swapTotal: 17179865088

    Backing Filesystem: btrfs

```

```
$> rpm -q podman
podman-4.9.5-150500.3.62.2.x86_64
```

```
$> uname -a
Linux my-server.ch 6.4.0-150700.53.25-default #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu Nov 27 12:47:48 UTC 2025 (3b7527f/lp) x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Running on [SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP7](https://documentation.suse.com/sles/15-SP7/) when I build my application images and run them through `podman-compose` then the exposed ports are reachable and works as expected.

Now I am in the process of having a simple CI/CD pipeline using GitHub [self hosted runners](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/concepts/runners/self-hosted-runners) where the action-runner is running under the same user identity and with:

```
- name: Dump environment context
  run: |
    echo "User: $(whoami)"
    echo "UID: $(id -u)"
    echo "XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR"
    podman info | grep rootless
    podman info | grep networkBackend
```

I get:

```
UID: 26974
XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/26974
    rootless: true
  networkBackend: netavark
  networkBackendInfo:
```

Which is exactly the same output of the same unix user starting the action-runners [`./run.sh`](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/how-tos/manage-runners/self-hosted-runners/add-runners#checking-that-your-self-hosted-runner-was-successfully-added-1) in terminal.

The problem is:

1 - After the pipeline is done I see the exact same containers in terminal shell, but their exposed ports are not accessible, e.g I get:

```
> curl -u 'user:pwd' -F 'foo.csv=@./bar.csv' http://localhost:8080/manual-upload-server/upload
curl: (7) Failed to connect to localhost port 8080 after 0 ms: Could not connect to server
```

Which doesn't happen to me when booting up the container inside the shell prompt.

2 - If I run the pipeline a second time then the attempt to stop the preexisting containers first fails with errors such as:

```
podman rm my-container
Error: cannot remove container 9cc8858207e38bf44891b00a7d7c7572d1d5cdb1d754ba86fc806678cb157386 as it is stopping - running or paused containers cannot be removed without force: container state improper
exit code: 2
``` 

All of which do not happen to me while working on terminal.

Unfortunately on SLES 15 SP7 the podman version you get is `4.9.5` and I don't know if / how to get and install a newer version to see if the same errors as above are still reproducible or not.

And both on terminal as well as inside my pipeline I do exactly the same to build / start / stop:

```
podman-compose --podman-build-args='--format docker' -f deploy/container-compose.yaml build --no-cache

podman-compose -f deploy/container-compose.yaml up -d

podman-compose -f deploy/container-compose.yaml down
```

### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Hi, thanks for reporting this issue.

You are using an older version of Podman (`4.9.5`) that is no longer supported. We only support the latest versions and require issues to be reproducible on builds from the `main` branch. Additionally, since you are using a Podman package from a Linux distribution (SUSE), please use their official support channels, unless you are able to reproduce the problem on upstream `main`.

Furthermore, you are using `podman-compose`, which is a separate project. Please report issues related to it directly in the [`podman-compose` repository](https://github.com/containers/podman-compose).

[SUPPORT.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/SUPPORT.md)

Because of these factors, I am going to close this issue. If you believe this is still a bug in Podman, please try to isolate the problem and provide a minimal, step-by-step reproducer using the latest upstream version.

For anyone else running into the same problem, following the solution I came up with: When I ran `podman` or `podman-compose` to build, stop, start or rebuild the containers, then their execution were bound to the shell prompt of that given GitHub action runner which gets cleaned up as soon as the action execution is accomplished after which GitHub then cleans up the corresponding resources and with that [the runtime monitor](https://github.com/containers/conmon) as one can see by the screenshot below: The solution was to simply come up with a `systemd` service, in my case [at the user level](https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man5/systemd.service.5.html) which wraps the whole `podman` or `podman-compose` invocations in the context of a user level `systemd` service which is not bound to the shell prompt context provided by GitHub runner anymore.
